- Propose a clear staged payment schedule (e.g., deposit, progress payments, final payment) linked to project milestones to manage cash flow effectively.
- Always verify local council requirements and obtain necessary permits or engineering plans before quoting to avoid unexpected delays and costs.
- Offer optional add-ons like lighting, fans, or screening to enhance the patio and potentially increase the project value.
- Conduct a thorough site visit to assess ground conditions, existing structures, and access, as these factors significantly impact labour and material costs.

### How to Quote a Shed Build in Australia

URL: https://quotemateapp.au/articles/how-to-quote-a-shed-build/

Master quoting shed builds in Australia with this detailed guide. Learn about typical costs, materials, labour, and common mistakes to win more jobs.

#### What to Include in Your Shed Quote

A comprehensive shed quote goes beyond just the structure itself. Clearly detail the shed's specifications, including dimensions, materials (e.g., Lysaght COLORBOND® steel or Zincalume®), and foundation type (e.g., concrete slab, piers). Itemise all labour for site preparation, erection, and any fit-out like internal partitioning or shelving. Crucially, specify inclusions and exclusions such as earthworks, concrete supply and pour, electrical fit-out, plumbing, council permits, and engineering plans, to avoid any client misunderstandings later. Finally, outline your payment schedule, warranty information, and terms and conditions.

#### Typical Costs for Shed Builds in Australia

Shed build costs vary significantly based on size, complexity, and materials. A small garden shed (3x3m) might range from $2,000 - $6,000 for a kit and basic installation, excluding a concrete slab. Medium workshop sheds (6x6m to 9x9m) typically cost between $10,000 - $35,000, including a decent concrete slab and professional erection. Larger rural or industrial sheds (12x12m+) can easily range from $30,000 to over $100,000, depending on custom features, engineering, and site challenges. Always factor in material costs, labour rates, and any sub-contractor fees (e.g., concreters at $80-$150 per square metre for a standard slab, or electricians) to provide an accurate estimate.

#### Materials Pricing and Sourcing

The bulk of your material costs will be for steel framing, sheeting, and the concrete slab. For quality steel products like roofing and wall cladding, consider suppliers like Lysaght, Stratco, or local steel fabricators, comparing prices for COLORBOND® or Zincalume® options. Timber components, fasteners, gutters, downpipes, and personal access doors can often be sourced competitively from hardware giants like Bunnings Warehouse or specialist building suppliers. Always get multiple quotes for significant material orders, especially for structural steel and concrete, as prices can fluctuate. Remember to factor in delivery costs and lead times, particularly for custom-fabricated components.

#### Labour Allocation and Efficiency Tips

Accurately estimating labour is key to profitability. Allocate time for every stage: initial site setup, earthworks (if applicable), foundation pouring, frame erection, cladding, roofing, and any internal fit-out. Consider the number of tradies required and their hourly rates, typically ranging from $60-$100 per hour for skilled workers. Complex designs, challenging site access, or working at heights will increase labour hours. Using power tools efficiently and having all materials readily available can significantly reduce erection time. For specialised tasks like intricate concreting or electrical wiring, factor in the costs of engaging qualified sub-contractors.

#### Common Mistakes to Avoid in Shed Quoting

One of the most frequent errors is underestimating site preparation, especially if the ground is uneven or requires extensive drainage. Failing to thoroughly check local council regulations and secure necessary permits or engineering plans (referencing the National Construction Code and AS/NZS 1170 for wind loads) can lead to costly delays and rework. Another pitfall is not detailing what's excluded from the quote, which can cause disputes with clients. Always account for potential access issues on-site and factor in a contingency for unforeseen circumstances like adverse weather, ensuring your quote protects your margins and reputation.

Pro tips:
- Always conduct a thorough on-site inspection to assess access, ground conditions, and existing services before quoting.
- Break down your quote into clear, itemised sections so the client can easily understand what they're paying for.
- Utilise digital quoting apps like QuoteMate to create professional, detailed quotes quickly and efficiently, including photos and drawings.
- Factor in a contingency of 5-10% for unforeseen costs or minor variations that might arise during the build.
- Clearly state your lead times for materials and project completion, managing client expectations from the outset.
- Keep up-to-date with local council requirements and Australian Standards (e.g., AS/NZS 1170 for structural design actions) to ensure compliance and avoid penalties.

### How to Quote a Driveway Gate Installation in Australia

URL: https://quotemateapp.au/articles/how-to-quote-a-driveway-gate-installation/

Master the art of quoting driveway gate installations in Australia. This guide covers costs, materials, labour, and common pitfalls for tradies.

#### What to Include in Your Driveway Gate Quote

A comprehensive quote clearly outlines the entire scope of work, starting with whether the gate will be manual or automated, and a swing or sliding design. Detail all materials, including the gate panel type, posts, hardware, and any automation system components. Don't forget to specify site preparation like excavation or existing fence removal, installation labour, electrical work if applicable (always by a licensed electrician, referencing AS/NZS 3000), and crucial finishes such as galvanising or powder coating. Always include your warranty, payment terms, and the quote's validity period to ensure transparency.

#### Typical Driveway Gate Installation Costs in Australia

The total cost for a driveway gate installation in Australia can vary significantly based on complexity and materials. Expect manual swing gates to range from $2,500 to $6,000+, while manual sliding gates typically cost $3,500 to $8,000+. Automated swing gates, requiring motors and electrical work, generally fall between $6,000 and $15,000+, and automated sliding gates are often $7,000 to $20,000+. High-end custom designs, intricate fabrication, or difficult site conditions can push these prices even higher, so a detailed site assessment is paramount for accurate pricing.

#### Materials Pricing – Getting Your Supply Chain Right

Material costs are a significant component of any gate quote, so smart sourcing is key. Steel gate panels can range from $1,000-$5,000+, while aluminium options might be $1,500-$7,000+, and timber gates $1,000-$4,000+, sourced from local fabricators or suppliers like Stratco. Steel posts usually cost $100-$400 each, and aluminium posts $150-$500 each. Automation kits from brands like BFT, FAAC, or CENTSYS typically run $700-$3,000+ for motors, with additional hardware, safety beams, and electrical components adding $300-$1,500+. Don't forget finishes like galvanising ($200-$800) or powder coating ($300-$1,200) that protect and enhance the gate's longevity.

#### Labour Tips for Efficient Driveway Gate Quotes

Accurately estimating labour time is crucial for profitability. Factor in time for the initial site assessment, any custom gate fabrication, and the installation itself, which includes digging, setting posts, mounting the gate, wiring the automation, and rigorous testing. Tradie hourly rates in Australia typically range from $60-$120/hour depending on location and specialisation, with potential additional costs for apprentices or labourers. Streamline your workflow by ensuring all materials are on site and pre-fabricating components where possible, remembering that any electrical work must be performed by a licensed professional to meet Australian standards.

#### Common Quoting Mistakes to Avoid

Underestimating site preparation is a frequent pitfall; always account for uneven ground, rock excavation, existing fence removal, or difficult access. Neglecting electrical requirements, such as power supply to the gate, conduit, safety switches, or the need for a licensed electrician (mandated by AS/NZS 3000), can lead to costly delays and rework. Don't forget 'hidden' costs like delivery fees, rubbish removal, unexpected material shortages, or the need for rectification work. Finally, a vague scope of work can cause client disputes, so clearly specify gate type, finish, automation features, and any exclusions, while also ensuring compliance with standards like AS 1926.1-2012 if the gate provides access to a pool area.

Pro tips:
- Always conduct a thorough site visit to assess terrain, access, power availability, and precise client expectations, as this forms the backbone of an accurate quote.
- Document everything meticulously in your quote, from materials and dimensions to automation features and warranty, preventing misunderstandings and scope creep.
- Factor in a contingency of 10-15% for unforeseen issues like unexpected ground conditions, material price fluctuations, or minor adjustments.
- Stay informed about relevant Australian Standards, particularly AS/NZS 3000 for electrical work and AS 1926.1-2012 for pool gates, ensuring compliance and client safety.
- Leverage technology like QuoteMate to streamline your quoting process, create professional, detailed quotes quickly, and track project profitability effortlessly.
- Build strong relationships with local suppliers and automation providers; this can lead to better pricing, faster delivery, and reliable technical support.

### How to Quote Gutter Replacement in Australia

URL: https://quotemateapp.au/articles/how-to-quote-gutter-replacement/

Master gutter replacement quotes for Australian tradies. Learn about costs, materials, and labour to win more jobs with QuoteMate.

#### What to Include in Your Gutter Replacement Quote

A comprehensive gutter replacement quote needs to clearly outline all components of the job. Start with a detailed site inspection covering linear metres of guttering, the number and location of downpipes, and any associated fascia or timber repairs needed. Specify the chosen material, colour, and profile, along with waste removal costs and any special access equipment like scaffolding or EWP. Don't forget to include a clear timeline and warranty information, adhering to Australian Consumer Law and relevant building codes.

#### Typical Gutter Replacement Costs in Australia

Gutter replacement costs vary significantly based on location, property size, and material choice. For a standard single-storey home, expect to quote between $35-$65 per linear metre, while double-storey or complex roofs can push this to $60-$120 per linear metre due to increased labour and safety requirements. A complete job for an average 3-bedroom house (approx. 50-70 linear metres) might range from $2,500 to $7,000, depending on downpipe replacements, fascia condition, and site accessibility. Always factor in potential unforeseen issues like rotted timber or asbestos considerations.

#### Materials Pricing and Australian Suppliers

Material costs are a major component. Zincalume gutters typically range from $10-$20 per linear metre, while popular Colorbond steel (e.g., from Lysaght or Stramit) costs $15-$30 per linear metre, offering a wider colour range and durability. PVC options, often used for downpipes, are generally $5-$15 per linear metre. Remember to price for accessories like brackets, joiners, stop ends, and pop-rivets, usually an additional 10-15% of the guttering cost. Source quality products from reputable suppliers like Bunnings Trade, Metroll, or local plumbing suppliers for competitive pricing and Australian Standard compliance.

#### Labour Estimations and Safety Considerations

Labour is often the largest cost, typically ranging from $60-$100 per hour per tradie, depending on experience and location. A standard gutter replacement for an average home might require 1-3 days for a two-person team. Crucially, factor in safety equipment like fall protection systems (e.g., harnesses, guardrails) and scaffolding or Elevated Work Platforms (EWPs) for multi-storey homes, which can add $500-$1,500 to the job cost. Always comply with Safe Work Australia guidelines and state-specific WHS regulations (e.g., WorkSafe Victoria, SafeWork NSW) to ensure a safe work environment and avoid penalties.

#### Common Quoting Mistakes to Avoid

A common mistake is underquoting due to inadequate site assessment, failing to account for hidden timber rot in fascias or sub-fascias, which can significantly increase material and labour costs. Another pitfall is not clearly detailing inclusions and exclusions, leading to client disputes or scope creep. Neglecting to factor in waste removal, travel time, or the cost of obtaining permits for scaffolding in some council areas can also eat into your profits. Always add a contingency (5-10%) for unexpected issues and communicate these possibilities clearly to the client upfront, potentially with a 'variations' clause.

Pro tips:
- Always conduct a thorough, on-site inspection, taking detailed photos and measurements of the existing guttering, downpipes, and fascia condition.
- Specify exact material types, colours, and profiles, referencing major Australian supplier names where possible (e.g., Colorbond 'Surfmist' from Lysaght).
- Clearly itemise all components: guttering, downpipes, brackets, waste removal, and any necessary repairs or safety equipment.
- Include a clause for unforeseen issues, especially hidden timber rot, and how these will be handled and priced as a variation.
- Factor in all safety equipment costs, including fall protection and scaffolding, from the outset to ensure compliance and worker safety.
- Use QuoteMate to generate professional, itemised quotes quickly, allowing for easy adjustments, digital client approvals, and accurate job tracking.

### How to Quote an Epoxy Floor Coating in Australia

URL: https://quotemateapp.au/articles/how-to-quote-an-epoxy-floor-coating/

Master epoxy floor coating quotes for Australian tradies. Learn about materials, labour, typical costs, and common mistakes to ensure profitable projects.

#### What to Include in Your Epoxy Floor Quote

A comprehensive quote needs to detail the entire scope of work, starting with surface preparation like grinding and repairs, through to the application of primers, base coats, decorative elements (if any), and clear topcoats. Clearly itemise materials, labour hours, equipment hire, and any specific site conditions such as moisture testing (essential for compliance with manufacturer specifications and avoiding future issues). Always include your payment terms, warranty information, and a disclaimer for unforeseen site conditions discovered during preparation to ensure transparency and manage client expectations effectively.

#### Typical Costs for Epoxy Floor Coatings in Australia

The cost of an epoxy floor coating in Australia can vary significantly based on the system's complexity, desired finish, and project size. For a basic, single-colour epoxy, expect to quote between $40-$80 per square metre. Decorative systems like flake or metallic epoxy typically range from $80-$150+ per square metre, reflecting the increased material and labour intensity. Industrial-grade, high-performance systems with specific chemical resistance or non-slip requirements can push costs higher, often exceeding $150 per square metre, depending on the number of coats and specialised additives required.

#### Materials Pricing – Getting Your Supply Chain Right

Accurate material pricing is crucial. Key components include primers ($5-$10/m²), base coats ($10-$25/m²), decorative elements like vinyl flakes ($2-$15/m²) or metallic pigments ($5-$25/m²), and robust clear topcoats ($5-$15/m²). Don't forget consumables such as grinding diamonds, rollers, brushes, mixing paddles, and safety gear. Establish relationships with major Australian suppliers like Hychem, Sika, Parchem, All Purpose Coatings, or Nutech Paint, as they offer trade discounts and technical support, ensuring you get quality products that meet Australian standards and project specifications.

#### Labour Tips for Efficient Epoxy Projects

Estimating labour accurately requires a thorough understanding of the project's nuances. Factor in time for extensive surface preparation (grinding, patching, vacuuming), which can be 50% or more of the total labour. Consider the number of coats, each requiring specific application and cure times before the next can be applied. An experienced applicator might charge $60-$90 per hour, so accurately calculating total man-hours for prep, mixing, application, and clean-up is vital. Account for potential after-hours work or restricted site access, which can increase labour costs.

#### Common Quoting Mistakes to Avoid

One of the biggest mistakes is underestimating surface preparation – poor prep leads to adhesion failures and costly re-dos. Failing to account for moisture vapour transmission in the slab (requiring a moisture barrier) or neglecting necessary crack repairs can also lead to project blowouts. Another common error is inaccurate material calculations, resulting in either costly over-ordering or project delays due to shortfalls. Always factor in adequate cure times between coats and before handover, as rushing the process compromises durability and can invalidate warranties. Lastly, forgetting minor consumables or disposal fees can erode your profit margin.

Pro tips:
- Always conduct a thorough site inspection to assess slab condition, moisture levels (using a moisture meter), and access challenges before quoting.
- Factor in adequate surface preparation – grinding, cleaning, and crack repairs are paramount for long-lasting adhesion and are often underestimated.
- Clearly itemise all components in your quote: surface prep, materials, labour, equipment hire, and any specialised treatments like non-slip additives.
- Specify cure times in your quote, informing the client exactly when the floor will be trafficable and fully cured, managing their expectations.
- Consider offering tiered options (e.g., a standard finish, a flake system, or a metallic finish) to cater to different budgets and client preferences.
- Utilise quoting software like QuoteMate to ensure all line items are included, calculations are accurate, and your quotes look professional and detailed.

### How to Quote a Laundry Renovation in Australia

URL: https://quotemateapp.au/articles/how-to-quote-a-laundry-renovation/

Master laundry renovation quotes for Australian tradies. Learn about costs, materials, labour, and avoiding common mistakes to win more jobs.

#### What to Include in Your Laundry Renovation Quote

A comprehensive laundry renovation quote starts with a detailed scope of work. Itemise everything from demolition and waste removal to new plumbing rough-in, electrical alterations, plastering, tiling, cabinetry installation, and final fixture fittings. Clearly separate labour costs, material costs, and any subcontractor expenses. Importantly, your quote should also detail your payment schedule, warranty information, and explicit exclusions to manage client expectations and prevent disputes down the track. Always reference adherence to relevant Australian standards like AS/NZS 3500 for plumbing and AS/NZS 3000 for electrical work.

#### Typical Costs for Australian Laundry Renovations

Laundry renovation costs in Australia can vary significantly. For a basic refresh, expect to quote between $5,000 - $10,000, which usually covers new paint, a basic tub, tapware, and a laminate benchtop. A mid-range renovation, involving new custom cabinetry, a tiled splashback, and some plumbing/electrical relocations, typically falls between $10,000 - $20,000. High-end or luxury laundries, featuring stone benchtops, premium appliances, and extensive layout changes, can range from $20,000 to $40,000+, with labour generally making up 40-50% and materials 40-50% of the total project cost. Always factor in a contingency for unforeseen issues, typically around 10-15% of the total cost.

#### Materials Pricing: What to Factor In

Material costs are a significant component of any laundry renovation. Cabinetry options vary widely: laminate flat-pack units from suppliers like Bunnings might cost $150-$300 per lineal metre, while custom-built melamine or two-pack cabinetry from local suppliers can range from $400-$800+ per lineal metre. Benchtops range from basic laminate ($80-$150/lm) to engineered stone ($300-$600/lm) or natural stone ($400-$1000+/lm). Tiling costs depend on the tile type, from budget ceramics ($20-$60/sqm) at Beaumont Tiles or Amber, to premium porcelain or feature tiles ($60-$150+/sqm). Don't forget fixture pricing for tubs ($200-$700), tapware (Reece, Tradelink $100-$500), and any appliance provisions.

#### Smart Labour Quoting and Scheduling Tips

When quoting labour, break down each task into specific hours or days for your team and any subcontractors. Clearly define roles for demolition, plumbing (licensed plumber required for AS/NZS 3500 compliance), electrical (licensed electrician for AS/NZS 3000 compliance), carpentry, plastering, and tiling. Standard hourly rates for skilled tradies in Australia typically range from $60-$100. Effective scheduling is crucial; coordinate trades to minimise downtime between stages and ensure a smooth workflow. Always factor in preparation and clean-up time, and consider a slightly higher rate for urgent or complex jobs to account for increased effort and potential overtime.

#### Common Quoting Mistakes to Avoid

One of the biggest mistakes is underquoting, which can lead to financial losses and client dissatisfaction. Always conduct a thorough site inspection to identify potential issues like existing water damage, asbestos, or complex plumbing/electrical relocations before finalising your quote. Poor communication regarding the scope, inclusions, and exclusions can also create major headaches, so get everything in writing. Failing to account for potential variations or not including a contingency buffer will leave you exposed. Lastly, relying on manual calculations can introduce errors; using a professional quoting app like QuoteMate can streamline the process, ensure accuracy, and present a polished quote to your clients.

Pro tips:
- Always perform a detailed on-site assessment before quoting to identify hidden issues and accurately scope the project.
- Get all agreements in writing, clearly outlining the scope, materials, payment terms, and project timeline.
- Educate your clients on potential variations and hidden costs, like asbestos removal or unexpected plumbing repairs, upfront.
- Include a 10-15% contingency in your quote to cover unforeseen challenges or minor adjustments without impacting your profit.
- Utilise a professional quoting app like QuoteMate to create consistent, detailed, and visually appealing quotes quickly.
- Clarify who is responsible for waste disposal and include this cost in your quote to avoid last-minute disputes.

## Getting paid

QuoteMate handles the full payment cycle for sole traders and small trade businesses in Australia. Quote on the spot, take a deposit before you start, send the invoice when the job's done, accept the payment via Square (Tap to Pay on iPhone or NFC-capable Android, or a hosted online payment link), and let automated reminders chase the slow ones. The total cost of acceptance is around 3.1% in-person and 2.9% online — or zero if you turn on the surcharge passthrough and let the customer pay the fee. This hub covers each stage and how to set it up properly.

### Tap to Pay on iPhone for Tradies

URL: https://quotemateapp.au/get-paid/tap-to-pay-iphone-tradies/

Tap to Pay on iPhone lets you accept Visa, Mastercard, Amex and Apple Pay payments by tapping a customer's card or phone on the back of your iPhone — no reader, no terminal, no Bluetooth pairing. QuoteMate uses Square's Mobile Payments SDK so the feature also works on NFC-capable Android phones, plus Google Pay. For Australian tradies who still rely on portable EFTPOS terminals, paper invoices, or 'I'll bank-transfer you tonight' (which becomes Saturday, then never), it's the biggest payments shift since contactless launched.

#### What you need to use Tap to Pay in QuoteMate

Three things: a supported phone (iPhone XS or later on iOS 16.4+, or any NFC-capable Android), a connected Square account against your ABN, and the QuoteMate app. There's no reader to buy and no monthly hardware fee. Square verifies the seller (one-time KYC), the feature lights up inside QuoteMate's payment sheet, and you can take a card or contactless payment within about 30 seconds of finishing a job. Manual key-in is also supported on every device for the rare card that won't tap.

#### Why tradies are switching from portable EFTPOS

Standalone EFTPOS terminals cost $20–$50 a month in rental, need their own SIM or Wi-Fi, run flat at the worst possible time, and add another piece of gear to the ute. Tap to Pay removes the hardware entirely. Square's published rate for in-person Tap to Pay in Australia is 1.6% per transaction — lower than the rates typically charged for keyed-in or online payments. Funds usually clear into your linked bank account the next business day. For a tradie doing 5 jobs a week at $800 average, that's the difference between getting paid this week and chasing payment for the next fortnight.

#### What it actually costs through QuoteMate

Two costs apply on every Tap to Pay transaction: Square's processing fee (1.6% in-person in Australia) and QuoteMate's platform fee (1.5% in-person), for a total cost of acceptance of around 3.1%. If you'd rather not eat the fee, you can switch on the surcharge passthrough — QuoteMate adds 2.9% to the customer's total at the payment step (clearly labelled), so you net the full quoted amount. The 2.9% cap keeps the surcharge under ACCC's excessive-surcharge threshold for any realistic card type, so you stay compliant without doing the maths yourself.

#### How to take a payment in QuoteMate

Open the invoice on your phone, tap 'Take Payment', tap 'Card on this device', and have the customer hold their card or phone against the back of yours. The transaction completes in a couple of seconds, the invoice is automatically marked as paid, the customer receives an emailed receipt, and the GST is logged for your BAS. You don't have to remember to update anything manually — Square's webhook reconciles the payment status in the background.

FAQ:

Q: Is Tap to Pay on iPhone available in Australia?
A: Yes — Tap to Pay on iPhone launched in Australia in 2023 and is supported by Square, Tyro, CommBank Smart, and others. QuoteMate uses Square's Mobile Payments SDK, which works for any Australian ABN on iPhone XS or later (iOS 16.4+) and NFC-capable Android.

Q: Does it work on Android too?
A: Yes. QuoteMate's Tap to Pay support runs through Square's Mobile Payments SDK on both iOS and Android, so any NFC-capable Android phone can accept contactless payments and Google Pay.

Q: What are the fees?
A: Square's standard Australian rate for in-person Tap to Pay is 1.6%. QuoteMate adds a 1.5% platform fee, for a total cost of acceptance of about 3.1% per transaction. Or enable the surcharge passthrough — QuoteMate adds 2.9% to the customer's total so you net the full quoted amount. There are no monthly hardware or rental fees either way.

Q: Can I pass the card fee on to my customer?
A: Yes. Turn on 'Surcharge payment fees' in QuoteMate's Square settings — a 2.9% surcharge is added to the customer's total at the payment step and clearly disclosed. The cap keeps you under ACCC excessive-surcharge rules for any realistic card type.

Q: How fast does the money hit my bank?
A: Square typically deposits funds into your linked bank account the next business day. Square also offers same-day deposits for an extra fee if you need cash flow faster.

### How to Chase Unpaid Invoices in Australia

URL: https://quotemateapp.au/get-paid/chasing-unpaid-invoices/

An unpaid invoice is one of the most expensive problems in trade. Time spent chasing is time not earning, and the longer an invoice sits past due, the lower the chance you'll ever collect it. The good news is there's a clear escalation path that resolves most cases inside 30 days without lawyers and without burning the relationship.

#### Day 1–7: Friendly reminder

Most overdue invoices aren't deliberate — clients forget, lose the email, or are waiting on their own customer to pay. A polite SMS or email on day 1 past due, then a follow-up at day 7, resolves the majority of cases. Keep the tone neutral and assume good faith. QuoteMate sends these automatically with templates that mention the invoice number, amount, due date, and a one-click payment link, so the client can pay without digging up the original invoice.

#### Day 14–30: Formal demand

If two reminders haven't worked, escalate to a formal letter of demand. State the original invoice details, total amount owing, any late-payment interest if specified in your terms, and a final pay-by date (usually 7 days). Reference your right to apply interest under the Late Payment of Commercial Debts standard (typically 8%–10% p.a. for B2B work) if you included it in your original quote. Most disputes get resolved at this stage because the client now sees you're documenting the trail.

#### Day 30+: Tribunals and debt collection

For amounts under $25,000 (NSW NCAT, VIC VCAT) or $20,000 (QLD QCAT), small claims tribunals are cheap, fast, and don't require a lawyer. Filing fees are around $50–$200 and you can usually get a hearing inside 8 weeks. For larger debts or commercial work, a debt collection agency (typically 10%–25% commission) is faster than court. The key is to have your trail documented: original quote, signed acceptance, invoice, reminders, demand letter. QuoteMate keeps all of this in one place per client.

FAQ:

Q: Can I charge interest on overdue invoices in Australia?
A: Yes, if it was specified in your original quote or contract terms. Standard rates are 8%–10% per annum for B2B debts. For consumer (residential) work, you'll need this clearly stated upfront.

Q: What's the cheapest way to recover an unpaid invoice?
A: Send a formal letter of demand first. If that doesn't work, your state's small claims tribunal (NCAT, VCAT, QCAT, etc.) is cheaper and faster than court for amounts under $20,000–$25,000.

Q: Should I use a debt collector?
A: For larger debts ($5,000+) or commercial clients, yes — debt collectors usually charge 10%–25% commission but are faster than court. For smaller residential debts, a tribunal is usually better value.

Q: How does QuoteMate help with unpaid invoices?
A: QuoteMate sends automated reminders at customisable intervals (e.g., 1 day, 7 days, 14 days past due), each with a one-click Square-hosted payment link. It also keeps the full audit trail (quote, acceptance, invoice, reminders) in one place if you ever need to escalate.

### Taking Deposits Legally in Australia

URL: https://quotemateapp.au/get-paid/taking-deposits-australia/

Asking for a deposit before starting a job is completely legal in Australia — but the maximum amount is capped by state-based home building laws. Get this wrong and you can be hit with fines, forced refunds, or have your contractor licence reviewed. The good news: the rules are simple once you know them.

#### Maximum deposits by state

For domestic building work, the typical legal maximums are: NSW (Home Building Act) — 10% for jobs over $20,000, no cap for smaller jobs but reasonableness applies; VIC (Domestic Building Contracts Act) — 10% for contracts under $20,000, 5% for contracts $20,000+; QLD (QBCC) — 10% for jobs over $3,300, 20% for off-site (prefab) components; SA (Building Work Contractors Act) — generally 10%; WA (Home Building Contracts Act) — 6.5% for contracts $7,500+; ACT — 10% for residential building work over $12,000; TAS — no statutory cap but consumer law fairness rules apply. Always check your state regulator's current rules before the contract is signed.

#### Deposits vs. progress payments

A deposit is the up-front payment that secures the job, locks in your schedule, and covers initial material orders. Progress payments are the staged payments you receive as the job hits milestones (e.g., demo complete, frame up, lock-up, completion). For trade work over $20,000, structuring progress payments correctly is more important than the deposit size — it's how you stay cash-flow-positive on long jobs without being out-of-pocket on materials.

#### How to take a deposit safely

Three rules. First, never take a deposit before the customer has accepted the quote in writing — a signed quote, an emailed acceptance, or a digital signature inside an app like QuoteMate is fine. Second, issue a deposit invoice that itemises what the deposit covers (materials, scheduling, etc.) and clearly references the parent quote. Third, pay the deposit straight into your business account, not personal. QuoteMate handles all three automatically: when a customer accepts a quote, it generates the deposit invoice and takes the payment via Square — Tap to Pay on the spot, or a hosted online link the customer pays from their phone — and logs everything against the job. If you'd rather not absorb the card fee, the optional 2.9% surcharge passthrough adds it to the customer's total.

FAQ:

Q: Can I ask for a 50% deposit?
A: Generally no, not for domestic building work. Most Australian states cap deposits at 5%–10% of the contract value. Always check your state's current home building regulator (NSW Fair Trading, VBA in Victoria, QBCC in Queensland, etc.).

Q: What if the deposit cap is too low to cover materials?
A: Use progress payments. For a $30,000 job, take a 5%–10% deposit on signing, then schedule progress payments at milestones (e.g., materials delivered, work commenced, halfway, completion). This keeps you cash-flow-positive without breaking deposit law.

Q: Do deposit caps apply to commercial work?
A: Most state laws apply specifically to 'domestic building work' for residential customers. Commercial contracts have more flexibility but should still be governed by clear written terms.

Q: Can I take a deposit on the spot via card?
A: Yes — using Tap to Pay through Square on iPhone or NFC-capable Android, you can take a deposit by card the moment the customer accepts the quote. The deposit invoice is auto-generated and the payment is logged automatically. If you'd rather not absorb the card fee, the optional 2.9% surcharge passthrough adds it to the customer's total.

### Automated Payment Reminders for Tradies

URL: https://quotemateapp.au/get-paid/automated-payment-reminders/

Manually chasing invoices is one of the most expensive habits in trade. A 30-minute reminder phone call at $90/hour effective rate is $45 of unpaid time per chase, and most clients need 2–3 nudges. Automated reminders sent via email and SMS resolve the same invoices without using a minute of your day, and the data shows they get paid faster than manual follow-ups.

#### The reminder cadence that works

Four touchpoints handle 90% of overdue invoices: a polite reminder one day before the due date ('just a heads up — your invoice is due tomorrow'), a friendly nudge the day after the due date ('quick reminder — your invoice is now overdue'), a firmer follow-up at 7 days past due, and a final notice at 14 days past due that mentions next steps. Each one should include the invoice number, amount, original due date, and a one-click payment link. QuoteMate runs this cadence automatically — you set it once and never think about it again.

#### Email vs. SMS reminders

Use both. SMS reminders have an average open rate of around 95% within minutes; email is closer to 25% within a day. The combination roughly doubles the chance of getting paid on the first reminder. The catch is that SMS costs money to send (typically a few cents per message) and you should never send more than 2–3 SMS per overdue invoice — anything more crosses the line into nuisance and can damage the relationship. QuoteMate's default cadence uses email for early reminders and switches to SMS for the firmer notices.

#### Templates that actually get a response

Three things make a reminder convert: a clear subject line that mentions the invoice number, a polite but specific tone (no passive-aggression, but no vague 'just checking in' either), and a payment link that takes the customer one tap away from settling. Avoid attaching a PDF of the invoice — most people don't open them on phones. Instead, summarise in plain text: 'Hi {first name}, this is a reminder that invoice #{number} for ${amount} was due {date}. You can pay here: {link}.' QuoteMate uses templates like this and personalises them automatically.

FAQ:

Q: How often should I send payment reminders?
A: A 4-touch cadence works for most jobs: 1 day before due, 1 day after due, 7 days after due, 14 days after due. After that, escalate to a formal letter of demand.

Q: Will automated reminders annoy my customers?
A: Not if the cadence is sensible. Reminders that include a one-click payment link are usually appreciated — most clients want to pay, they just forget. Avoid sending more than 2 SMS per overdue invoice.

Q: Can I customise the reminder schedule per client?
A: Yes — in QuoteMate you can override the default cadence for specific clients (e.g., a long-standing commercial client on net-30 terms) or for individual invoices.

Q: Does QuoteMate send reminders by SMS?
A: Yes. SMS reminders are included in the Pro plan and use your business name as the sender. Email reminders are included in all plans, including the free trial.

## Comparisons vs other Australian / NZ tradie apps

### QuoteMate vs Tradify

URL: https://quotemateapp.au/compare/tradify/

What Tradify is: Tradify is a job management platform for trade businesses covering quoting, scheduling, timesheets, and invoicing.

Tradify pricing: Tradify starts at $35/user/month with per-user pricing that scales up for teams.

Feature comparison:
- AI-powered quoting — QuoteMate: Yes | Tradify: No
- Real-time supplier pricing — QuoteMate: Yes | Tradify: No
- Professional PDF quotes — QuoteMate: Yes | Tradify: Yes
- Invoicing — QuoteMate: Yes | Tradify: Yes
- Job scheduling — QuoteMate: No | Tradify: Yes
- Timesheets — QuoteMate: No | Tradify: Yes
- Offline mode — QuoteMate: Yes | Tradify: No
- Voice-to-text quoting — QuoteMate: Yes | Tradify: No
- Flat pricing (no per-user fees) — QuoteMate: $29/month | Tradify: $35/user/month
- Free plan available — QuoteMate: Yes | Tradify: No

Bottom line: Tradify is a solid job management platform for larger teams that need scheduling and timesheets. QuoteMate is the better choice if your priority is fast, accurate quoting with AI-powered material suggestions and real-time supplier pricing — at a fraction of the cost.

### QuoteMate vs ServiceM8

URL: https://quotemateapp.au/compare/servicem8/

What ServiceM8 is: ServiceM8 is a field service management app covering job management, quoting, scheduling, invoicing, and client communication.

ServiceM8 pricing: ServiceM8 plans range from $0 to $379/month depending on job volume and features.

Feature comparison:
- AI-powered quoting — QuoteMate: Yes | ServiceM8: No
- Real-time supplier pricing — QuoteMate: Yes | ServiceM8: No
- Professional PDF quotes — QuoteMate: Yes | ServiceM8: Yes
- Invoicing — QuoteMate: Yes | ServiceM8: Yes
- Job scheduling & dispatch — QuoteMate: No | ServiceM8: Yes
- Client communication (SMS/email) — QuoteMate: Share via SMS/email/WhatsApp | ServiceM8: Yes
- Offline mode — QuoteMate: Yes | ServiceM8: Yes
- Voice-to-text quoting — QuoteMate: Yes | ServiceM8: No
- Simple flat pricing — QuoteMate: $29/month | ServiceM8: $0–$379/month
- Australian supplier integration — QuoteMate: Major suppliers | ServiceM8: No

Bottom line: ServiceM8 is a comprehensive field service platform ideal for businesses managing crews and complex scheduling. QuoteMate is purpose-built for fast, accurate quoting — describe any job and get an AI-generated materials list with live supplier pricing, all for a simple flat rate.

### QuoteMate vs Fergus

URL: https://quotemateapp.au/compare/fergus/

What Fergus is: Fergus is a job management platform designed for trade businesses, offering quoting, scheduling, timesheets, purchase orders, and invoicing.

Fergus pricing: Fergus pricing starts at $55/month for basic features, scaling up for larger teams.

Feature comparison:
- AI-powered quoting — QuoteMate: Yes | Fergus: No
- Real-time supplier pricing — QuoteMate: Yes | Fergus: No
- Professional PDF quotes — QuoteMate: Yes | Fergus: Yes
- Invoicing — QuoteMate: Yes | Fergus: Yes
- Job scheduling — QuoteMate: No | Fergus: Yes
- Purchase orders — QuoteMate: No | Fergus: Yes
- Offline mode — QuoteMate: Yes | Fergus: No
- Voice-to-text quoting — QuoteMate: Yes | Fergus: No
- Flat pricing — QuoteMate: $29/month | Fergus: From $55/month
- Free plan available — QuoteMate: Yes | Fergus: No

Bottom line: Fergus offers deep job management features including purchase orders and detailed job costing. QuoteMate wins on speed and simplicity — create accurate quotes with AI material suggestions and real-time pricing in under 2 minutes, at about half the monthly cost.

### QuoteMate vs ChatGPT (and other AI chatbots)

URL: https://quotemateapp.au/compare/chatgpt/

What ChatGPT (and other AI chatbots) is: ChatGPT, Google Gemini, Microsoft Copilot, and Anthropic Claude are general-purpose AI chatbots. Tradies sometimes paste a job description in and ask for a "quote", but the result is a chat reply — not a branded PDF, not an invoice, and not grounded in current Australian supplier pricing.

ChatGPT (and other AI chatbots) pricing: ChatGPT Plus, Gemini Advanced, and Claude Pro are each ~$30 AUD/month — and you still need a separate quoting app to actually send the quote.

Feature comparison:
- Generates a job description — QuoteMate: AI-assisted, voice-to-text | ChatGPT (and other AI chatbots): Yes
- Live, accurate supplier prices (Bunnings, Reece, Tradelink, etc.) — QuoteMate: Yes | ChatGPT (and other AI chatbots): Hallucinated / outdated
- Consistent output between runs — QuoteMate: Reusable templates | ChatGPT (and other AI chatbots): Different every time
- Branded PDF quote with your logo & ABN — QuoteMate: Yes | ChatGPT (and other AI chatbots): No
- Convert quote to invoice in one tap — QuoteMate: Yes | ChatGPT (and other AI chatbots): No
- Customer can accept the quote from email — QuoteMate: Yes | ChatGPT (and other AI chatbots): No
- Take card payment on-site (tap-to-pay) — QuoteMate: Yes | ChatGPT (and other AI chatbots): No
- Send Australian payment links (PayID, BPAY, bank transfer) — QuoteMate: Yes | ChatGPT (and other AI chatbots): No
- Push to Xero — QuoteMate: Yes | ChatGPT (and other AI chatbots): No
- Auto GST & ABN handling — QuoteMate: Built in | ChatGPT (and other AI chatbots): Manual, prone to error
- Track sent / accepted / paid status — QuoteMate: Yes | ChatGPT (and other AI chatbots): No
- Works offline on remote sites — QuoteMate: Yes | ChatGPT (and other AI chatbots): No
- Pricing — QuoteMate: Free or $29/month Pro | ChatGPT (and other AI chatbots): ~$30/month + still need a quoting app

Bottom line: A general-purpose chatbot can help you brainstorm a job description, but it can't produce a quote you can stand behind on a contract. The prices are hallucinated, the output changes every time you ask, and there's no PDF, no payment, and no Xero. QuoteMate uses AI in the same way (describe a job, get a materials list) but grounds every line item in live Australian supplier pricing, produces a branded PDF, and gives you one-tap invoicing, payment, and Xero sync. If you want AI to help you quote, use AI built into a quoting app — not a chatbot.

### QuoteMate vs Excel & Google Sheets

URL: https://quotemateapp.au/compare/spreadsheets/

What Excel & Google Sheets is: Many tradies still quote in Excel, Google Sheets, or Numbers. It works for the first 10 jobs, but it doesn't scale: prices go stale, GST gets miscalculated, the file lives on one laptop, and you can't take payment from a spreadsheet.

Excel & Google Sheets pricing: Excel ~$9.95/month for Microsoft 365, Google Sheets free with a Google account.

Feature comparison:
- Branded PDF quotes — QuoteMate: Yes | Excel & Google Sheets: Manual setup
- AI material list generation — QuoteMate: Yes | Excel & Google Sheets: No
- Live supplier pricing (auto-updates) — QuoteMate: Yes | Excel & Google Sheets: No
- Reusable templates — QuoteMate: Built in | Excel & Google Sheets: Copy/paste a file
- Quote-to-invoice conversion — QuoteMate: One tap | Excel & Google Sheets: Manual rewrite
- Track sent / accepted / paid — QuoteMate: Yes | Excel & Google Sheets: No
- GST & ABN handling — QuoteMate: Automatic | Excel & Google Sheets: Manual formulas
- Online quote acceptance — QuoteMate: Yes | Excel & Google Sheets: No
- Take card payment on-site — QuoteMate: Yes | Excel & Google Sheets: No
- Xero integration — QuoteMate: One-tap sync | Excel & Google Sheets: Manual export
- Works on phone on-site — QuoteMate: Native app | Excel & Google Sheets: Awkward on mobile

Bottom line: A spreadsheet is fine until you realise your prices are six months stale, your last quote went out without GST, and your customer has nowhere to pay it. QuoteMate gives you all the flexibility of a spreadsheet (custom line items, your own labour rates, your own markup) but adds AI material generation, live supplier pricing, branded PDFs, online acceptance, payment links, and Xero sync. Tradies who switch from spreadsheets typically save 1–3 hours per quote and start charging GST correctly.

### QuoteMate vs Invoice2go

URL: https://quotemateapp.au/compare/invoice2go/

What Invoice2go is: Invoice2go is a general-purpose invoicing app used across many industries, offering estimates, invoices, and payment tracking.

Invoice2go pricing: Invoice2go starts at $5.99/month for basic invoicing, up to $39.99/month for the premium plan.

Feature comparison:
- AI-powered quoting — QuoteMate: Yes | Invoice2go: No
- Real-time supplier pricing — QuoteMate: Yes | Invoice2go: No
- Trade-specific templates — QuoteMate: Yes | Invoice2go: No
- Professional PDF quotes — QuoteMate: Yes | Invoice2go: Yes
- Invoicing — QuoteMate: Yes | Invoice2go: Yes
- Payment tracking — QuoteMate: Yes | Invoice2go: Yes
- Offline mode — QuoteMate: Yes | Invoice2go: No
- Built for Australian tradies — QuoteMate: Yes | Invoice2go: No
- GST calculations — QuoteMate: Automatic | Invoice2go: Manual setup
- Voice-to-text quoting — QuoteMate: Yes | Invoice2go: No

Bottom line: Invoice2go is a capable general invoicing tool, but it's not built for tradies. QuoteMate understands trade jobs, suggests materials automatically, and integrates with Australian suppliers — making it faster and more accurate for quoting construction and trade work.

## Cities served (local pages)

QuoteMate has trade-by-city landing pages for every supported trade in: Sydney, NSW; Melbourne, VIC; Brisbane, QLD; Perth, WA; Adelaide, SA; Gold Coast, QLD; Canberra, ACT; Hobart, TAS; Newcastle, NSW; Wollongong, NSW.
URL pattern: https://quotemateapp.au/quotes-for-{trade}/{city}/
